fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_getConfig
fields.description: Gets the requested Recommender Config. There is only one instance of the config for each Recommender.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_list
fields.description: Lists recommendations for the specified Cloud Resource. Requires the recommender.*.list IAM permission for the specified recommender.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to return from this request. Non-positive values are ignored. If not specified, the server will determine the number of results to return.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. If present, retrieves the next batch of results from the preceding call to this method. `page_token` must be the value of `next_page_token` from the previous response. The values of other method parameters must be identical to those in the previous call.

args.name: filter
args.description: Filter expression to restrict the recommendations returned. Supported filter fields: * `state_info.state` * `recommenderSubtype` * `priority` * `targetResources` Examples: * `stateInfo.state = ACTIVE OR stateInfo.state = DISMISSED` * `recommenderSubtype = REMOVE_ROLE OR recommenderSubtype = REPLACE_ROLE` * `priority = P1 OR priority = P2` * `targetResources : //compute.googleapis.com/projects/1234/zones/us-central1-a/instances/instance-1` * `stateInfo.state = ACTIVE AND (priority = P1 OR priority = P2)` The max allowed filter length is 500 characters. (These expressions are based on the filter language described at https://google.aip.dev/160)

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommenderConfig
types.description: Configuration for a Recommender.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Name of recommender config. Eg, projects/[PROJECT_NUMBER]/locations/[LOCATION]/recommenders/[RECOMMENDER_ID]/config

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Fingerprint of the RecommenderConfig. Provides optimistic locking when updating.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Last time when the config was updated.

fields.name: revisionId
fields.description: Output only. Immutable. The revision ID of the config. A new revision is committed whenever the config is changed in any way. The format is an 8-character hexadecimal string.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Allows clients to store small amounts of arbitrary data. Annotations must follow the Kubernetes syntax. The total size of all keys and values combined is limited to 256k. Key can have 2 segments: prefix (optional) and name (required), separated by a slash (/). Prefix must be a DNS subdomain. Name must be 63 characters or less, begin and end with alphanumerics, with dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics between.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: A user-settable field to provide a human-readable name to be used in user interfaces.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommenderGenerationConfig
types.description: A Configuration to customize the generation of recommendations. Eg, customizing the lookback period considered when generating a recommendation.

fields.name: params
fields.description: Parameters for this RecommenderGenerationConfig. These configs can be used by or are applied to all subtypes.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1ListRecommendationsResponse
types.description: Response to the `ListRecommendations` method.

fields.name: recommendations
fields.description: The set of recommendations for the `parent` resource.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token that can be used to request the next page of results. This field is empty if there are no additional results.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1Recommendation
types.description: A recommendation along with a suggested action. E.g., a rightsizing recommendation for an underutilized VM, IAM role recommendations, etc

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Name of recommendation.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Free-form human readable summary in English. The maximum length is 500 characters.

fields.name: recommenderSubtype
fields.description: Contains an identifier for a subtype of recommendations produced for the same recommender. Subtype is a function of content and impact, meaning a new subtype might be added when significant changes to `content` or `primary_impact.category` are introduced. See the Recommenders section to see a list of subtypes for a given Recommender. Examples: For recommender = "google.iam.policy.Recommender", recommender_subtype can be one of "REMOVE_ROLE"/"REPLACE_ROLE"

fields.name: lastRefreshTime
fields.description: Last time this recommendation was refreshed by the system that created it in the first place.

fields.name: additionalImpact
fields.description: Optional set of additional impact that this recommendation may have when trying to optimize for the primary category. These may be positive or negative.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Fingerprint of the Recommendation. Provides optimistic locking when updating states.

fields.name: associatedInsights
fields.description: Insights that led to this recommendation.

fields.name: xorGroupId
fields.description: Corresponds to a mutually exclusive group ID within a recommender. A non-empty ID indicates that the recommendation belongs to a mutually exclusive group. This means that only one recommendation within the group is suggested to be applied.

fields.name: targetResources
fields.description: Fully qualified resource names that this recommendation is targeting.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1Impact
types.description: Contains the impact a recommendation can have for a given category.

fields.name: service
fields.description: The service that this impact is associated with.

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_list_recommendations_items_primaryImpact_category
types.description: Category that is being targeted.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1CostProjection
types.description: Contains metadata about how much money a recommendation can save or incur.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration for which this cost applies.

types.name: GoogleTypeMoney
types.description: Represents an amount of money with its currency type.

fields.name: currencyCode
fields.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

fields.name: units
fields.description: The whole units of the amount. For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

fields.name: nanos
fields.description: Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount. The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive. If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero. If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative. If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero. For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1SecurityProjection
types.description: Contains various ways of describing the impact on Security.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Additional security impact details that is provided by the recommender.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1SustainabilityProjection
types.description: Contains metadata about how much sustainability a recommendation can save or incur.

fields.name: kgCO2e
fields.description: Carbon Footprint generated in kg of CO2 equivalent. Chose kg_c_o2e so that the name renders correctly in camelCase (kgCO2e).

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration for which this sustainability applies.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1ReliabilityProjection
types.description: Contains information on the impact of a reliability recommendation.

fields.name: risks
fields.description: Reliability risks mitigated by this recommendation.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Per-recommender projection.

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_list_recommendations_items_priority
types.description: Recommendation's priority.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommendationContent
types.description: Contains what resources are changing and how they are changing.

fields.name: operationGroups
fields.description: Operations to one or more Google Cloud resources grouped in such a way that, all operations within one group are expected to be performed atomically and in an order.

fields.name: overview
fields.description: Condensed overview information about the recommendation.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1OperationGroup
types.description: Group of operations that need to be performed atomically.

fields.name: operations
fields.description: List of operations across one or more resources that belong to this group. Loosely based on RFC6902 and should be performed in the order they appear.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1Operation
types.description: Contains an operation for a resource loosely based on the JSON-PATCH format with support for: * Custom filters for describing partial array patch. * Extended path values for describing nested arrays. * Custom fields for describing the resource for which the operation is being described. * Allows extension to custom operations not natively supported by RFC6902. See https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6902 for details on the original RFC.

fields.name: action
fields.description: Type of this operation. Contains one of 'add', 'remove', 'replace', 'move', 'copy', 'test' and custom operations. This field is case-insensitive and always populated.

fields.name: resourceType
fields.description: Type of GCP resource being modified/tested. This field is always populated. Example: cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/Project, compute.googleapis.com/Instance

fields.name: resource
fields.description: Contains the fully qualified resource name. This field is always populated. ex: //cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/projects/foo.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Path to the target field being operated on. If the operation is at the resource level, then path should be "/". This field is always populated.

fields.name: sourceResource
fields.description: Can be set with action 'copy' to copy resource configuration across different resources of the same type. Example: A resource clone can be done via action = 'copy', path = "/", from = "/", source_resource = and resource_name = . This field is empty for all other values of `action`.

fields.name: sourcePath
fields.description: Can be set with action 'copy' or 'move' to indicate the source field within resource or source_resource, ignored if provided for other operation types.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Value for the `path` field. Will be set for actions:'add'/'replace'. Maybe set for action: 'test'. Either this or `value_matcher` will be set for 'test' operation. An exact match must be performed.

fields.name: pathFilters
fields.description: Set of filters to apply if `path` refers to array elements or nested array elements in order to narrow down to a single unique element that is being tested/modified. This is intended to be an exact match per filter. To perform advanced matching, use path_value_matchers. * Example: ``` { "/versions/*/name" : "it-123" "/versions/*/targetSize/percent": 20 } ``` * Example: ``` { "/bindings/*/role": "roles/owner" "/bindings/*/condition" : null } ``` * Example: ``` { "/bindings/*/role": "roles/owner" "/bindings/*/members/*" : ["x@example.com", "y@example.com"] } ``` When both path_filters and path_value_matchers are set, an implicit AND must be performed.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1ValueMatcher
types.description: Contains various matching options for values for a GCP resource field.

fields.name: matchesPattern
fields.description: To be used for full regex matching. The regular expression is using the Google RE2 syntax (https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax), so to be used with RE2::FullMatch

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_list_recommendations_items_content_operationGroups_items_operations_items_pathValueMatchers
types.description: Similar to path_filters, this contains set of filters to apply if `path` field refers to array elements. This is meant to support value matching beyond exact match. To perform exact match, use path_filters. When both path_filters and path_value_matchers are set, an implicit AND must be performed.

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1ValueMatcher2
types.description: Contains various matching options for values for a GCP resource field.

fields.name: matchesPattern
fields.description: To be used for full regex matching. The regular expression is using the Google RE2 syntax (https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax), so to be used with RE2::FullMatch

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommendationStateInfo
types.description: Information for state. Contains state and metadata.

fields.name: stateMetadata
fields.description: A map of metadata for the state, provided by user or automations systems.

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_list_recommendations_items_stateInfo_state
types.description: The state of the recommendation, Eg ACTIVE, SUCCEEDED, FAILED.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommendationInsightReference
types.description: Reference to an associated insight.

fields.name: insight
fields.description: Insight resource name, e.g. projects/[PROJECT_NUMBER]/locations/[LOCATION]/insightTypes/[INSIGHT_TYPE_ID]/insights/[INSIGHT_ID]

types.description: Configuration for an InsightType.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Fingerprint of the InsightTypeConfig. Provides optimistic locking when updating.

fields.name: updateTime

fields.name: revisionId
fields.description: Output only. Immutable. The revision ID of the config. A new revision is committed whenever the config is changed in any way. The format is an 8-character hexadecimal string.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Allows clients to store small amounts of arbitrary data. Annotations must follow the Kubernetes syntax. The total size of all keys and values combined is limited to 256k. Key can have 2 segments: prefix (optional) and name (required), separated by a slash (/). Prefix must be a DNS subdomain. Name must be 63 characters or less, begin and end with alphanumerics, with dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics between.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1Insight
types.description: An insight along with the information used to derive the insight. The insight may have associated recommendations as well.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Name of the insight.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Free-form human readable summary in English. The maximum length is 500 characters.

fields.name: targetResources
fields.description: Fully qualified resource names that this insight is targeting.

fields.name: insightSubtype
fields.description: Insight subtype. Insight content schema will be stable for a given subtype.

fields.name: content
fields.description: A struct of custom fields to explain the insight. Example: "grantedPermissionsCount": "1000"

fields.name: lastRefreshTime
fields.description: Timestamp of the latest data used to generate the insight.

fields.name: observationPeriod
fields.description: Observation period that led to the insight. The source data used to generate the insight ends at last_refresh_time and begins at (last_refresh_time - observation_period).

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Fingerprint of the Insight. Provides optimistic locking when updating states.

fields.name: associatedRecommendations
fields.description: Recommendations derived from this insight.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1InsightStateInfo
types.description: Information related to insight state.

fields.name: stateMetadata
fields.description: A map of metadata for the state, provided by user or automations systems.

types.description: Insight state.

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_insightTypes_insights_list_insights_items_category
types.description: Category being targeted by the insight.

types.name: query_recommender_projects_locations_insightTypes_insights_list_insights_items_severity
types.description: Insight's severity.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1InsightRecommendationReference
types.description: Reference to an associated recommendation.

fields.name: recommendation
fields.description: Recommendation resource name, e.g. projects/[PROJECT_NUMBER]/locations/[LOCATION]/recommenders/[RECOMMENDER_ID]/recommendations/[RECOMMENDATION_ID]

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_updateConfig
fields.description: Updates a Recommender Config. This will create a new revision of the config.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: The list of fields to be updated.

args.name: validateOnly
args.description: If true, validate the request and preview the change, but do not actually update it.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_markDismissed
fields.description: Mark the Recommendation State as Dismissed. Users can use this method to indicate to the Recommender API that an ACTIVE recommendation has to be marked back as DISMISSED. MarkRecommendationDismissed can be applied to recommendations in ACTIVE state. Requires the recommender.*.update IAM permission for the specified recommender.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_markClaimed
fields.description: Marks the Recommendation State as Claimed. Users can use this method to indicate to the Recommender API that they are starting to apply the recommendation themselves. This stops the recommendation content from being updated. Associated insights are frozen and placed in the ACCEPTED state. MarkRecommendationClaimed can be applied to recommendations in CLAIMED, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or ACTIVE state. Requires the recommender.*.update IAM permission for the specified recommender.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_markSucceeded
fields.description: Marks the Recommendation State as Succeeded. Users can use this method to indicate to the Recommender API that they have applied the recommendation themselves, and the operation was successful. This stops the recommendation content from being updated. Associated insights are frozen and placed in the ACCEPTED state. MarkRecommendationSucceeded can be applied to recommendations in ACTIVE, CLAIMED, SUCCEEDED, or FAILED state. Requires the recommender.*.update IAM permission for the specified recommender.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_recommenders_recommendations_markFailed
fields.description: Marks the Recommendation State as Failed. Users can use this method to indicate to the Recommender API that they have applied the recommendation themselves, and the operation failed. This stops the recommendation content from being updated. Associated insights are frozen and placed in the ACCEPTED state. MarkRecommendationFailed can be applied to recommendations in ACTIVE, CLAIMED, SUCCEEDED, or FAILED state. Requires the recommender.*.update IAM permission for the specified recommender.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_insightTypes_updateConfig
fields.description: Updates an InsightTypeConfig change. This will create a new revision of the config.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: The list of fields to be updated.

args.name: validateOnly
args.description: If true, validate the request and preview the change, but do not actually update it.

fields.name: recommender_projects_locations_insightTypes_insights_markAccepted
fields.description: Marks the Insight State as Accepted. Users can use this method to indicate to the Recommender API that they have applied some action based on the insight. This stops the insight content from being updated. MarkInsightAccepted can be applied to insights in ACTIVE state. Requires the recommender.*.update IAM permission for the specified insight.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommenderConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for a Recommender.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Identifier. Name of recommender config. Eg, projects/[PROJECT_NUMBER]/locations/[LOCATION]/recommenders/[RECOMMENDER_ID]/config

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Fingerprint of the RecommenderConfig. Provides optimistic locking when updating.

inputFields.name: updateTime
inputFields.description: Last time when the config was updated.

inputFields.name: revisionId
inputFields.description: Output only. Immutable. The revision ID of the config. A new revision is committed whenever the config is changed in any way. The format is an 8-character hexadecimal string.

inputFields.name: annotations
inputFields.description: Allows clients to store small amounts of arbitrary data. Annotations must follow the Kubernetes syntax. The total size of all keys and values combined is limited to 256k. Key can have 2 segments: prefix (optional) and name (required), separated by a slash (/). Prefix must be a DNS subdomain. Name must be 63 characters or less, begin and end with alphanumerics, with dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics between.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: A user-settable field to provide a human-readable name to be used in user interfaces.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1RecommenderGenerationConfig_Input
types.description: A Configuration to customize the generation of recommendations. Eg, customizing the lookback period considered when generating a recommendation.

inputFields.name: params
inputFields.description: Parameters for this RecommenderGenerationConfig. These configs can be used by or are applied to all subtypes.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1MarkRecommendationDismissedRequest_Input
types.description: Request for the `MarkRecommendationDismissed` Method.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Fingerprint of the Recommendation. Provides optimistic locking.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1MarkRecommendationClaimedRequest_Input
types.description: Request for the `MarkRecommendationClaimed` Method.

inputFields.name: stateMetadata
inputFields.description: State properties to include with this state. Overwrites any existing `state_metadata`. Keys must match the regex `/^a-z0-9{0,62}$/`. Values must match the regex `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]{0,255}$/`.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Required. Fingerprint of the Recommendation. Provides optimistic locking.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1MarkRecommendationSucceededRequest_Input
types.description: Request for the `MarkRecommendationSucceeded` Method.

inputFields.name: stateMetadata
inputFields.description: State properties to include with this state. Overwrites any existing `state_metadata`. Keys must match the regex `/^a-z0-9{0,62}$/`. Values must match the regex `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]{0,255}$/`.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Required. Fingerprint of the Recommendation. Provides optimistic locking.

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1MarkRecommendationFailedRequest_Input
types.description: Request for the `MarkRecommendationFailed` Method.

inputFields.name: stateMetadata

inputFields.name: etag

inputFields.name: etag

inputFields.name: updateTime

types.name: GoogleCloudRecommenderV1MarkInsightAcceptedRequest_Input
types.description: Request for the `MarkInsightAccepted` method.

inputFields.name: stateMetadata
inputFields.description: Optional. State properties user wish to include with this state. Full replace of the current state_metadata.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Required. Fingerprint of the Insight. Provides optimistic locking.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
